intro.title: What Is an AI Baby Generator?

content.title: Create a fictional portrait from photo references
content.text: An AI baby generator creates a fictional baby portrait from one or two reference photos. Start with two separate portraits, one clear couple photo, or one single portrait, then choose a style and edit the prompt. The result is a creative image for entertainment and sharing. It does not predict genetics, parentage, health, or what a real child will look like.

howToUse.title: How to Create an AI Baby Portrait

steps.title: Upload one or two clear photos
steps.desc: Add two separate portraits for a clear two-person reference, one couple photo where both faces are easy to see, or one portrait for a single-reference result.

steps.title: Choose a portrait style
steps.desc: Pick Soft Studio, Cozy Blanket, Sunny Outdoor, or another preset. Each style fills in a useful prompt that remains editable.

steps.title: Generate, review, and download
steps.desc: Create the portrait, review it in the result panel, and adjust the style or prompt if you want a different mood before downloading.

photoTips.title: Photo Tips for Better Baby Portraits
photoTips.subtitle: Clear references help the generator read faces, lighting, and framing before it creates a fictional portrait.

items.title: Keep faces easy to see
items.desc: Use front-facing or near-front-facing photos with visible eyes, nose, and mouth. Avoid sunglasses, masks, and tiny faces.

items.title: Use balanced light
items.desc: Natural window light or soft indoor light usually preserves more useful facial detail than strong shadows or colored lighting.

items.title: Match the framing
items.desc: For two separate photos, similar head-and-shoulders crops are easier to read than one close selfie and one distant full-body image.

items.title: Check a couple photo
items.desc: Make sure both faces are large, unobstructed, and sharp. Avoid crowded group photos where the intended references are unclear.

testimonials.title: How People Use the AI Baby Generator
testimonials.subtitle: Three practical workflows for creating and refining a fictional baby portrait.

items.name: Maya R.
items.quote: We used two simple phone portraits and tried the studio and outdoor styles. The second result worked better after I removed the busy background instructions. We treated it as a fun image to send each other, not something that was supposed to be accurate.

items.name: Jordan L.
items.quote: I wanted a gentle placeholder image for a private card concept before the family chose real photography. The Family Card preset gave me useful framing, and I could change the clothing and background without rebuilding the prompt.

items.name: Lena S.
items.quote: The clearest result came from two photos with similar lighting. I switched from a realistic studio look to a softer storybook style. Some details changed between generations, so I chose the portrait with the cleanest expression.

faq.q: How does the AI baby generator work?
faq.a: Upload one or two reference photos, choose a Baby Portrait Style, and edit the prompt if needed. Toolaze uses the photos and prompt to create a fictional baby portrait that you can review and download.
Yes. You can use one clear single-person portrait or one couple photo. Two separate portraits usually give the workflow clearer references, but they are not required.

faq.q: Can I upload two separate parent photos or one couple photo?
faq.a: Yes. The tool accepts up to two images. You can upload two individual portraits, one couple photo with both faces visible, or one single portrait.
Use clear, front-facing or near-front-facing photos with balanced light and visible facial features. Avoid sunglasses, heavy filters, strong shadows, tiny faces, and crowded group photos.

faq.q: Is the AI baby generator free and online?
faq.a: Yes. You can open the tool online, create a baby portrait without signing up, and download the result without a watermark. Usage limits may vary.

faq.q: Does it predict what a real baby will look like?
faq.a: No. The result is a fictional image made for fun and creative use. It does not predict genetics, parentage, health, or a real child's future appearance.

faq.q: Can I choose a baby portrait style?
faq.a: Yes. Start with Soft Studio, Cozy Blanket, Sunny Outdoor, Playful Smile, Storybook Portrait, or Family Card. You can edit the prompt to change the expression, clothing, light, or background.

faq.q: Can I download the generated image?
faq.a: Yes. After the portrait appears in the result panel, review it and use the download action to save the image you prefer.
